provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 poster: 'Adults £14.40.'

items.d: A man or woman who is not a child.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The children are at the zoo.'
provenance.note: primary clue avoids headword; example clause uses it after the blank-style gloss

items.d: It says where you are. The children are here, ___ school.
items.hintDe: Sagt, wo du bist (zum Beispiel beim Zoo).

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.21 Everyday English: 'At last!'
provenance.note: mc are the other two everyday-English phrases plus a repeat slot trimmed in distractorPool

items.d: You speak this when you wait a long time and then it is here.
items.hintDe: Du sagst es nach langem Warten.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'The parrot is blue and yellow. It's beautiful.'

items.d: Very nice to look at.
items.s: The parrot is blue and yellow. It's ___.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The chair is behind the desk.'

items.d: It is at the back. You cannot look at it from the front.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'Look – there are three monkeys. They're behind the tree.'

items.d: It is at the back of a thing. It is not in front.
items.s: There are three monkeys. They're ___ the tree.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'There's a big giraffe.'

items.d: Not small. A giraffe is this.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 fact box: 'Dogs are welcome ... But they can't run around!'

items.d: It joins two ideas. I love cats, ___ not dogs.
items.s: Dogs are welcome. ___ they can't run around.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'The parrot is in the car.'

items.d: A thing with four wheels that you go in on the road.
items.hintDe: Hat vier Räder, fährt auf der Straße.

provenance.sbRef: WB Unit 2 ex.6: 'There ... four children.'

items.d: A young one, not an adult. You go to school when you are this.
items.s: There are four ___ in the wildlife park.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2: 'Buddy is a dog.'

items.d: An animal you love. Buddy is one.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'Buddy's family is at the zoo.'

items.d: It is your mum and dad and you.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 chant: 'here's a chant for you.'
provenance.note: primary clue avoids headword; example uses it after the gloss-style clue

items.d: It says who gets a thing. This is ___ you.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'Parking is free.'

items.d: You do not give money for it.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'They are from California.'
provenance.note: primary clue avoids headword; example uses it after the gloss-style clue

items.d: It says where your home is. They are here, ___ California.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'There's a big giraffe.'

items.d: A big animal at the zoo. It has a very long neck.
items.hintDe: Dieses Tier hat einen sehr langen Hals.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'Say hi to Grandma!'

items.d: She is your mum's mum.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'A group ticket is €8.90.'

items.d: Many children, not one child.
items.hintDe: Viele zusammen, nicht nur eine Person.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.17: 'Listen to the guide.'

items.d: A man or woman who walks with you and tells you about the zoo.
items.hintDe: Diese Person zeigt dir den Zoo.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'Buddy is happy.'

items.d: You feel good. You are not sad.
items.hintDe: Du fühlst dich gut, nicht traurig.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.18 model: 'He's in class 4A.'

items.d: It is for a boy or a man.
items.s: Mike is at the zoo. ___ is happy.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.21 Everyday English: 'How strange!'

items.d: You speak this for a very, very odd thing.
items.hintDe: Du sagst es, wenn etwas sehr seltsam ist.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.19: 'It's ... the car.'

items.d: It is not on the desk and not under it. The pen is here, ___ the box.
items.hintDe: Drinnen, zum Beispiel in der Box.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The tree is in front of you.'

items.d: It is here, you look at it. It is not behind you.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'The tree in front of you!'

items.d: Here, where you look. It is not behind you.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.21 Everyday English: 'Let me see.'

items.d: You speak this when you want to look at a thing.
items.hintDe: Du sagst es, wenn du etwas anschauen willst.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 poster: 'See a lion.'

items.d: A big animal at the zoo. It is the king of the animals.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'The giraffe has a long neck.'

items.d: Not short. A giraffe neck is this.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The parrot is next to the monkeys.'

items.d: A small animal that climbs in trees at the zoo.
items.s: The ___ is next to the parrot.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The parrot is next to the monkeys.'

items.d: It is here, very close. It is not behind and not under.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'It's next to the brown monkey.'

items.d: Very close, at the side. The parrot is here, close to the monkey.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'There's a book on my desk.'

items.d: It is at the top of a thing. The book is here, at the top of the desk.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'Look! The parrot is on Buddy now.'

items.d: A bird that can talk to you.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 poster: 'Feed the penguins.'

items.d: A black and white bird. It cannot fly, but it can swim.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'She is from England.'

items.d: It is for a girl or a woman.
items.hintDe: Für ein Mädchen oder eine Frau.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The monkey isn't big. It's small.'

items.d: Not big. A baby monkey is this.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.21: 'Colour your stone.'

items.d: It is grey and hard. You find it on the ground.
items.hintDe: Grau und hart, liegt am Boden.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'Rahim and Sue are 11. They are from Manchester.'

items.d: It is for more than one. Sue and Mike are two.
items.s: Don and Sue are 11. ___ are from Manchester.

provenance.sbRef: Master list carrier: 'Adult tickets are €14.40.'

items.d: You give money for this. Then you can go in.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 poster: 'Can I bring my dog, Buddy?'

items.d: To take a thing with you to a place.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 chant: 'We're not happy. Let us out.'
provenance.note: blank takes only the verb 'Let'; particle 'out' stays in the carrier so a single-token answer is grammatical

items.d: To open the door so a child can go out.
items.hintDe: Die Tür aufmachen und jemanden raus lassen.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.18: 'Talk about the boys and girls.'

items.d: To speak with a friend.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 chant context: 'I want a lion.'

items.d: To wish to have a thing.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'Let's go on a train.'

items.d: You go on this at the zoo. It is a long thing on tracks.
items.s: Go on a ___ at the zoo.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'The monkeys are behind the tree.'

items.d: It is a big green plant. A monkey can climb in it.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'There's a book under my desk.'
provenance.note: definition uses headword inside an example after the gloss-free clue; primary clue does not contain it

items.d: It is here, at the bottom. The book is here, ___ the desk.
items.hintDe: Darunter, zum Beispiel unter dem Tisch.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'there's a parrot under the giraffe!'

items.d: At the bottom of a thing. The parrot is here, and the giraffe is on top.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 chant: 'Let us out!'

items.d: It is for you and me. The guide can look at ___.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 chant: 'We are from York.'

items.d: It is for you and me.
items.s: Sue and I are at the zoo. ___ are happy.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 story: 'Where's the parrot?'

items.d: You ask this when you want to find a thing.
items.hintDe: Du fragst danach, wenn du etwas suchst.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 p.18: 'I'm in Year 7.'

items.d: You are ten of these now.
items.hintDe: Du bist zum Beispiel zehn davon alt.

provenance.sbRef: SB Unit 2 — MORE Words: 'There are many animals in the zoo.'

items.d: A place where you can see a lion, a monkey and a giraffe.
